page.content: The data presented in the accompanying table provides a comprehensive overview of the health outcomes associated with the specified variable within the population under study. Notably, the total figure stands at 336,785, accompanied by a 95% confidence interval ranging from 299,653 to 373,919.

page.content: This statistic underscores the significance of the variable in relation to public health, highlighting the prevalence within the community. Furthermore, the average annual rate, expressed per 100,000 individuals, is calculated at 10.1, indicating the rate at which the health outcome occurs within the specified population over time. This metric serves as a critical indicator for health policymakers and researchers, facilitating a deeper understanding of the trends and patterns associated with the variable. By analyzing both the total counts and the average annual rates, stakeholders can identify potential areas for intervention and allocate resources more effectively. The confidence intervals provided for both the total and percentage metrics reinforce the reliability of these figures, allowing for a more robust interpretation of the data. As such, this table not only provides essential quantitative insights but also sets the stage for further investigation and targeted health initiatives.
